name: math-explorer
description: Scouts one aspect of the run's problem and its approach space (the orchestrator assigns a lens): reads the statement, the knowledge base, and the live approach population; reports the terrain — distinct openings, what has been tried, what dead-ended and why, which techniques look promising. Several run in parallel each round, each a different lens. Does NOT attempt the proof.

name: proof-outliner
description: Puts up a field of rival approaches for the run's one problem: opens new approaches, revises stuck ones, nominates live ones to advance, or asks to copy one with two viable gap-fills — each a slugged skeleton with the hard steps identified, not a finished proof. Hands the field to the outline-reviewer, which ranks and narrows it. Call after the explorers, once per round.

name: outline-reviewer
description: The ranking gate. Reviews the outliner's field of rival approaches BEFORE any proof effort: cuts the doomed (wrong technique, unjustified leaps, missing cases, circular reasoning), registers approved new approaches, copies any the outliner asks to branch, ranks the whole field head-to-head, and emits the build set. Runs every round.

name: proof-builder
description: Owns one approach from the build set (the orchestrator assigns its slug) and fills its outline into a complete, rigorous prose proof: every step justified, every case settled, all computations shown. Writes to its own approach file. Builders run in parallel, one per slug. Call after the outline-reviewer emits the build set.

name: proof-reviewer
description: Adversarially judges a candidate proof for correctness, rigor, and progress. Hunts for gaps, unproven claims, and skipped cases; decides solved/partial/unsolved and returns an APPROVE / CHANGES REQUESTED / RETHINK verdict. Call after every proof-builder attempt.
